title: 4 Posting Outgoing AFT Files
body_text:
4	Posting Outgoing AFT Files
4.1	Overview of Posting Outgoing AFT Files
Outgoing AFT files are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s account according to the lead time and due date they are submitted. The offsetting transaction, if applicable, is posted to the originator芒聙聶s account on the due date. Originators are set up to receive this posting automatically for Canadian dollar files, but for US dollar files, the financial institution may have to manually post the offsetting transaction to the business member芒聙聶s account. Verify with your banking system service provider.
The total value of AFT transactions and the offsetting transaction are netted. If the net total entry is balanced, meaning 0, there is no posting. If the net total entry does not equal 0, either a debit or a credit entry is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. Credits and debits are coded as AO (AFT Outgoing Settlement).
4.2	Posting Outgoing Canadian Dollar AFT Files
Settlement for an AFT file submitted by a financial institution or business member originator is posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s account as follows:
File Delivered to Central 1 with Sufficient Lead Time (Due on Business Day)
Files delivered to Central 1 with sufficient lead time and a due date that is a business day, are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on the due date. For example, if the file is delivered Wednesday and the due date is Friday, the total will be posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on Friday.
File Delivered to Central 1 with Sufficient Lead Time (Due on Non-Business Day)
Files delivered to Central 1 with sufficient lead time and a due date that isn芒聙聶t a business day, are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on the business day following the due date. For example, if the file is delivered Wednesday and the due date is Saturday, the total will be posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on Monday.
File Delivered to Central 1 with Insufficient Lead Time
Files delivered to Central 1 with insufficient lead time are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on the business day that the file is received at Central 1. For example, if the file is delivered Wednesday but the due date is Wednesday or earlier, the total will be posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account on the date received or on the next business day.
Posting the Offsetting Transaction in the AFT File
The offsetting transaction is either included in Canadian dollar AFT files or it is created by Central 1. If the net amount is balanced, meaning 0, there is no entry posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account. If the net amount isn芒聙聶t balanced, either an offsetting transaction was not included in the file or a transaction was rejected. In this case, the financial institution can manually post the net amount to its internal account or the originator芒聙聶s account.
For some originators, AFT files do not include the offsetting transaction. To determine if the transaction was automatically posted, check the Settlement Register Report (ICSR/UCSR). If the report shows amounts in only the Due To or Due From part of the Our Receipts section, the file does not include the offsetting transaction. If amounts show on both sides, the offsetting transaction was automatically posted.
If the offsetting transaction isn芒聙聶t included in the file, the financial institution must manually post the file total to the originator芒聙聶s (Canadian or US) account. Proceed as follows:
Post the file total to the originator芒聙聶s account as a credit if transactions only appear on the ICSR/UCSR report under the Due To section. The financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account will have been credited. Also post the file total internally to debit the appropriate general ledger (GL) and credit the business member芒聙聶s account.
Post the file total to the originator芒聙聶s account as a debit if transactions only appear on the ICSR/UCSR report under the Due From section. The financial institution芒聙聶s Central 1 account will have been debited. Also post the file total internally to credit the appropriate GL and debit the business member芒聙聶s account.
Note:	See the AFT Reports Part for more information on the ICSR/UCSR report.
4.3	Posting Outgoing US Dollar AFT Files
Financial institution and business member originators can create US dollar AFT files as long as the transactions are destined for Canadian financial institutions. Outgoing US dollar AFT files are posted in the same manner as Canadian dollar files except that they are posted to the financial institution芒聙聶s US dollar Central 1 account, and if applicable, the offsetting transaction is posted to the originator芒聙聶s US dollar financial institution account. Credits and debits are coded as AO (AFT Outgoing Settlement).
For further information, see the following reports in the AFT Reports Part:
Daily AFT Clearing Settlement Report 芒聙聰 AFT Originating (ICPS/UCPS)
Settlement Register Report (ICSR/UCSR)
Account and Activity and Balances Report (AIBC/AIBU for BC or AIOC/AIOU for Ontario).
END
